strongest_supported_claim: Corrected independent proposals match tested HMC observables with shorter critical-region autocorrelation.
main_boundary: One 2D phi4 study; support tails and total-cost comparison remain open.

strongest_supported_claim: Path objectives can lower weight variance for unnormalized targets.
main_boundary: Discretization, training and benchmark transfer to lattice physics vary by method.

strongest_supported_claim: Learned independent proposals can be made asymptotically exact and symmetry aware.
main_boundary: Training/support cost and realistic QCD scaling are unresolved.

strongest_supported_claim: Learned layers improve nonequilibrium estimator quality while retaining path correction.
main_boundary: Protocol length and affected degrees of freedom can still dominate cost.

strongest_supported_claim: Symmetry, force regularization and cross-volume training improve sample quality.
main_boundary: Raw samples can remain biased; score evaluations and correction cost are substantial.

strongest_supported_claim: Sequential models plus local/block MH reproduce tested scalar/spin observables.
main_boundary: Small systems and no realistic gauge/fermion scaling.

strongest_supported_claim: Explicit scale separation improves near-critical large-volume sampling and enables variance reduction.
main_boundary: Only 2D scalar evidence; fine-level ESS still declines.

strongest_supported_claim: Mode-resolved diagnostics and controlled theory reveal failures hidden by aggregate metrics.
main_boundary: They diagnose or explain failure but are not complete samplers.
